(d) To submit to the Committee for its consideration and approval, as appropriate, a detailed programme of work in which the Monitoring Team will describe its planned activities in the discharge of its mandate, including its planned travel on behalf of the Committee;

(e) To collect, on behalf of the Committee, information on reported cases of non-compliance with the measures referred to in paragraph 1 of the present resolution, including, but not limited to, compiling information obtained from Member States, contacting the parties concerned and conducting case studies, on its own initiative as well as at the request of the Committee, and to make recommendations on cases of non-compliance considered for consideration by the Committee;

(f) To make recommendations to the Committee to assist Member States in the implementation of the measures referred to in paragraph 1 of the present resolution and in the preparation of their listing proposals;

(g) To assist the Committee in its consideration of the listing proposals, including by compiling and transmitting information on the proposed listing and by preparing the draft explanatory memorandum referred to in paragraph 20 of the present resolution;
(h) To bring to the attention of the Committee any new or relevant developments that may justify delisting, such as the publication of information on a deceased person;
(i) Consult with Member States prior to visiting some of them under the programme of work approved by the Committee;
(j) To urge Member States to submit additional names and identifiers to be included in the List, in accordance with the Committee's instructions;

(k) Consult with the Committee, the Government of Afghanistan or any Member State concerned, as appropriate, for the purpose of identifying persons or entities that may be added to or removed from the List;
(l) Provide the Committee with identification and other additional information to assist it in maintaining the List and ensuring that the information contained therein is as accurate as possible;

(m) Gather, evaluate and monitor information on, report on and make recommendations on the implementation of the measures, conduct case studies, as appropriate; and thoroughly study any other relevant issues as directed by the Committee;

(n) To consult with Member States and other relevant organizations and bodies, including UNAMA and other organizations of the United Nations system, and to engage in a continuous dialogue with their representatives in New York and capitals, taking into account their comments, including on issues that may be raised in the reports of the Monitoring Team referred to in paragraph (a) of the present annex;

(o) To cooperate closely with the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) and to engage in regular dialogue with Member States on the links between drug trafficking and individuals, groups, undertakings and entities to be listed in accordance with paragraph 1 of the present resolution, and to prepare the reports requested by the Committee;

(p) To submit to the Committee, on 1 December 2014, a special written report prepared, as appropriate, in consultation with the Government of Afghanistan, UNODC and the expert groups of the sanctions committees, containing examples of cooperation among criminal organizations, including groups involved in kidnapping for ransom, drug producers and dealers, as well as those who illegally exploit natural resources in Afghanistan, including precious and semi-precious stones, and individuals, groups, undertakings and entities that are to be included in the List pursuant to paragraph 1 of this resolution;

(q) To consult with the intelligence and security services of Member States, including within the regional framework, in order to facilitate the exchange of information and to improve the implementation of measures;

(r) To consult with relevant private sector representatives, including financial institutions, to learn about the practical modalities of the assets freeze and to develop recommendations for strengthening this measure;

(s) To cooperate closely with the Al-Qaida Sanctions Committee pursuant to resolutions 1267 (1999) and 1989 (2011) and other United Nations counter-terrorism bodies to provide information on measures taken by Member States with regard to kidnapping and hostage-taking for ransom and on trends and developments in this regard;

(t) Consult with the Government of Afghanistan, Member States, relevant private sector representatives, including financial institutions and interested non-financial professionals and enterprises, and relevant international organizations, including the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) and its regional bodies, to promote awareness and understanding of the modalities

text_fr: pratiques  du  gel  des  avoirs  et  d'élaborer  des  recommandations  aux  fins  du renforcement de cette mesure conformément à la recommandation 6 du GAFI sur le gel des avoirs et aux directives connexes;
text_en: asset freeze practices and develop recommendations for strengthening this measure in accordance with FATF recommendation 6 on asset freeze and related guidelines;

items_fr: u) Consulter  le  Gouvernement  afghan,  les  État  Membres,  les  représentants du  secteur  privé  concernés  et  les  autres  organisations  internationales,  dont l'Organisation  de  l'aviation  civile  internationale,  l'Association  du  transport  aérien international et l'Organisation mondiale des douanes, en vue de faire connaître et de mieux comprendre les modalités pratiques de l'interdiction de voyager et du gel des avoirs et d'élaborer des recommandations aux fins du renforcement de ces mesures;

v) Consulter  le  Gouvernement afghan,  les  État  Membres, les  organisations internationales et régionales et les représentants du secteur privé concernés au sujet de la menace que les engins explosifs improvisés font peser sur la paix, la sécurité et la  stabilité  en Afghanistan, en vue de faire connaître cette menace et de préconiser des mesures propres à la dissiper;
w) Collaborer avec les organisations internationales et régionales compétentes afin de faire mieux connaître et respecter les mesures;

x) Coopérer avec INTERPOL et les États Membres afin de se procurer des photographies et une description physique des personnes inscrites sur la Liste et, si la  législation  nationale  le  permet,  d'autres  données  biométriques  et  des  éléments biographiques à inclure dans les notices spéciales, et afin également d'échanger des informations sur les nouvelles menaces;

y) Aider  les  autres  organes  subsidiaires  du  Conseil  de  sécurité  et  leurs groupes d'experts, à leur demande, à intensifier leur coopération avec INTERPOL, comme le prévoit la résolution 1699 (2006);

z) Aider  le  Comité  à  fournir  aux  États  Membres,  sur  leur  demande,  une assistance  en  matière  de  renforcement  des  capacités  pour  leur  permettre  de  mieux mettre en œuvre les mesures;

aa) Faire  rapport  au  Comité,  à  intervalles  réguliers  ou  à  sa  demande,  en présentant des communications orales ou écrites sur ses travaux, y compris sur les visites qu'elle a effectuées auprès d'États Membres et sur ses activités;

bb) Étudier la nature de la menace que les personnes, groupes, entreprises et entités  associés  aux  Taliban  font  peser  sur  la  paix,  la  stabilité  et  la  sécurité  de l'Afghanistan  et  les  meilleurs  moyens  d'y  faire  face,  notamment  en  instaurant  des échanges avec des chercheurs, des établissements universitaires et des spécialistes, compte tenu des priorités établies par le Comité, et rendre compte à celui-ci de ses travaux;

cc) Réunir des informations, notamment auprès du Gouvernement afghan et d'autres  États  Membres,  sur  les  voyages  effectués  dans  le  cadre  des  dérogations accordées,  conformément  aux  paragraphes  13  et  14,  et  faire  rapport  au  Comité, selon qu'il conviendra;
dd) S'acquitter  de  toute  autre  responsabilité  que  le  Comité  pourrait  lui confier.
items_en: (u) To consult with the Government of Afghanistan, Member States, relevant private sector representatives and other international organizations, including the International Civil Aviation Organization, the International Air Transport Association and the World Customs Organization, with a view to raising awareness and understanding of the practical modalities of the travel ban and assets freeze and to developing recommendations for strengthening those measures;

(v) To consult with the Government of Afghanistan, Member States, relevant international and regional organizations and private sector representatives on the threat posed by improvised explosive devices to peace, security and stability in Afghanistan, with a view to publicizing the threat and advocating measures to address it;
(w) Collaborate with relevant international and regional organizations to raise awareness of and respect for measures;

(x) Cooperate with INTERPOL and Member States to obtain photographs and physical descriptions of listed individuals and, where permitted by national legislation, other biometric data and biographical elements to be included in special notices, and also to exchange information on emerging threats;

(y) To assist other subsidiary bodies of the Security Council and their groups of experts, at their request, in intensifying their cooperation with INTERPOL, as provided for in resolution 1699 (2006);
(z) Assisting the Committee in providing capacity-building assistance to Member States, at their request, to enable them to better implement the measures;
(aa) To report to the Committee at regular intervals or at its request by submitting oral or written communications on its work, including on its visits to Member States and on its activities;

(bb) To study the nature of the threat posed by individuals, groups, undertakings and entities associated with the Taliban to the peace, stability and security of Afghanistan and the best means of dealing with it, including through exchanges with researchers, academic institutions and specialists, taking into account the priorities established by the Committee, and to report to the Committee on its work;

(cc) To gather information, including from the Government of Afghanistan and other Member States, on travel under exceptions granted, in accordance with paragraphs 13 and 14, and to report to the Committee, as appropriate;
(dd) To carry out any other responsibilities that the Committee may assign to it.
